text.title: shadcn-ui-architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for shadcn-ui-architecture. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Keep `components/ui/` as vendored primitives only: no business logic, no feature-specific state. Feature compositions live in `components/<feature>/` and reference `ui/` primitives.
rule.rationale: Mixing business logic into primitives makes them reusable in name only; every feature requirement bleeds in and the component becomes untestable independently.

rule.statement: Treat `components/ui/*` as protected: gate via CODEOWNERS or a lint rule; require a separate explicit PR to modify them, never silent edits during feature work.
rule.rationale: UI primitive changes are blast-radius high — they affect every consumer. Silent agent edits during feature work are a top source of regressions.

rule.statement: Define all colors, radii, and spacing tokens in ONE place — `globals.css` `:root` and `.dark` blocks. Ban inline hex values (`#fff`, `bg-[#abc123]`) via an ESLint rule.
rule.rationale: Scattered hardcoded values make theme changes require a global search-replace; a single token source makes dark mode and brand updates a one-line change.

rule.statement: In dark mode, always use semantic token names (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--destructive`), never concrete Tailwind shades (`dark:bg-gray-800`).
rule.rationale: Concrete shades require per-component dark-mode overrides and drift across components; semantic tokens propagate theme changes automatically.

rule.statement: Use `cva` for variants; use `compoundVariants` for state combinations (`variant="outline" + size="sm"`) instead of adding new flat variants. Stop adding variants when count exceeds ~6 per prop.
rule.rationale: Flat variant explosion creates a junk-drawer component with 12+ variants that are impossible to maintain or visually verify.

rule.statement: Always export both the component and its `*Variants` CVA function (e.g., `buttonVariants`). Downstream code may need `cn(buttonVariants({ variant }))` for non-button elements.
rule.rationale: Exporting only the component hides the variant contract from compositions that style non-semantic elements to match.

rule.statement: All primitive components must use `React.forwardRef`. Add `ComponentName.displayName = 'ComponentName'` immediately after the `forwardRef` call.
rule.rationale: Without `forwardRef`, ref wiring breaks for Radix `asChild` slots. Without `displayName`, React DevTools shows `_c2` and agent debugging is impossible.
